name: Bi Gan
description: Bi Gan, also known as Prince Gan, was a prominent figure in Chinese mythology and history, revered as a symbol of loyalty, integrity, and righteousness. He served as an advisor to the tyrannical King Zhou of the Shang Dynasty. Bi Gan was known for his unwavering honesty and courage, often criticizing the king's corrupt and decadent behavior, even at great personal risk. Legend states that Bi Gan possessed an extraordinary heart, often described as having seven apertures, which allowed him to perceive truth and justice with unparalleled clarity. His unwavering commitment to ethical governance eventually led to his tragic demise. King Zhou, influenced by his consort Daji, who was actually a fox spirit in disguise, became increasingly enraged by Bi Gan's constant admonishments. Daji convinced the king that Bi Gan's heart, with its unique properties, could be used to cure her supposed illness. The king, blinded by his infatuation and cruelty, ordered Bi Gan to be executed and his heart extracted. According to some versions of the myth, Bi Gan's heart was taken, yet he miraculously survived and fled the capital, becoming the ancestor of the Lin family. He is often depicted as a scholar or official, sometimes holding his heart in his hands, a poignant reminder of his sacrifice. Bi Gan is venerated as a patron saint of merchants, particularly those who value honesty and fair dealing. His story serves as a powerful reminder of the importance of moral courage and the consequences of tyranny and corruption. His legacy continues to inspire those who stand up for what is right, even in the face of adversity.
subtitle: The Loyal and Righteous Advisor

keyMyths.title: Bi Gan's Advice to King Zhou
keyMyths.description: Bi Gan consistently advised King Zhou against his tyrannical and corrupt practices, earning the king's ire. He pleaded for the king to prioritize the well-being of his people over personal indulgence.

keyMyths.title: The Extraction of Bi Gan's Heart
keyMyths.description: Daji, a fox spirit in disguise, convinced King Zhou that Bi Gan's heart could cure her illness. The king ordered Bi Gan's execution and the extraction of his heart, a symbol of his cruelty.

keyMyths.title: Bi Gan's Flight and Legacy
keyMyths.description: Despite having his heart removed, Bi Gan miraculously survived and fled the capital. He became the ancestor of the Lin family and a symbol of unwavering integrity.

sources.author: Xu Zhonglin
sources.description: A major source for the myths surrounding Bi Gan and the fall of the Shang Dynasty.
sources.text: A major source for the myths surrounding Bi Gan and the fall of the Shang Dynasty.

sources.author: Various
sources.description: Record the lineage tracing back to Bi Gan and his significance to the Lin surname.
